scope.goal: Repair the v2180 renderer parity comparison surface after GitHub Actions exposed an unnormalized source decision digest embedded in a prerequisite-intake notes line.
nonGoals: No managed audit connection or runtime execution enablement

parallelProjects.java: recommended parallel; v2181 changes only Node test normalization and does not require Java to wait
parallelProjects.miniKv: recommended parallel; v2181 changes only Node test normalization and does not require mini-kv to wait

ciFailure.rootCause: The v2180 parity normalizer already folded rendered digest entries, but missed a notes phrase rendered as 'The prerequisite intake is chained to <source decision digest>.'. That source digest is derived from upstream archive content and can differ between Windows CRLF checkout content and Linux LF checkout content while preserving the same public renderer structure.

passed.command: npx vitest run test/rendererMigrationV2167Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2168Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2169Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2170Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2175Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2176Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2178Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2179Parity.test.ts test/rendererMigrationV2180Parity.test.ts --maxWorkers=2

passed.result: 2 test files passed, 4 tests passed; v2181 walkthrough counted 3002 Chinese characters and satisfied the current quality gate
1 test file passed, 1 test passed under v8 coverage instrumentation; thresholds were set to 0 only because this focused run intentionally does not execute the full suite
passed with 0 errors and 263 existing warnings

safetyBoundary.publicOutput: No public Markdown output was changed. Only normalized test comparison strings changed.
safetyBoundary.testSurface: The normalizer now replaces the source decision digest in the prerequisite-intake notes sentence while keeping decision states, checks, blockers, section headings, and business text hash-sensitive.
safetyBoundary.stopCondition: If another CI-only drift appears, inspect the exact rendered line and add the narrowest comparison normalizer rule; do not change fixtures or product renderer output to satisfy parity.
